description: Sphere Sidecar — Extraction Rulebook for Volunteer LLM Agents

context.whatIsSphere: Sphere is a living knowledge graph. Nodes are born (incarnated), accumulate heat through evaluations, and eventually decay into fossils. The strongest nodes ascend to amber (permanent). Your job as a volunteer is to extract knowledge from a coding session and produce NodeSeeds — the raw material for new Sphere nodes.
context.whatIsNodeSeed: A NodeSeed is a pre-incarnation node. It has tags (spatial coordinates), a summary (visible from afar), and optional content (visible on focus). After you submit NodeSeeds, the incarnation pipeline handles vectorization, flag classification, and database storage.
context.whatIsContribute: Your output gets packaged into an ExperienceCapsule and submitted to POST /sphere/contribute. The incarnation pipeline (Gatekeeper → Parser → Tagger → Packer → Bookkeeper) does the rest. You do NOT need to set metrics (h/w/d) — Packer assigns initial metrics based on tier.

nodeSeedFormat.description: Each extracted knowledge unit becomes a NodeSeed.

schema.summary: string — Brief headline of the discovery (20-200 chars). This gets vectorized for spatial position and SHA256-hashed for dedup. Write it like a search result title.
schema.tags: string[] — 2-5 lowercase hyphenated keywords. Used by Tagger for 16-bit classification flags and by vector search for spatial positioning.
schema.content: string (optional) — Detailed explanation visible when a user focuses on this node. Include implementation details, rationale, gotchas. Omit for trivial nodes.
schema.flags: number — Set to 0. The Tagger derives proper 16-bit flags from your tags.

examples.summary: Implemented pull-based volunteer queue for distributed LLM extraction in Gateway
examples.content: Created volunteer-queue.ts with enqueue/poll/submit lifecycle. Reaper handles timeout (5min claim, 10min pending). Overflow protection spills oldest 25% to Extractor when queue exceeds 100 items. FIFO ordering ensures fair distribution.

examples.summary: Fixed JWT token refresh to auto-renew before session expiry with 5-minute window
examples.content: Token middleware now checks expiry before each API call. If within 5 minutes of expiry, triggers background refresh. Handles timezone edge cases in expiry comparison.

examples.summary: Chose Dual Timeout pattern (idle + maxWait) for IngestBuffer batching
examples.content: Idle timeout (2s) flushes when activity stops. MaxWait (10s) provides absolute upper bound. Capacity trigger (20 items or 50KB) for burst protection. Follows Periphery EntryBuffer pattern.

tagTaxonomy.description: Tags determine a node's spatial position and classification flags. Combine 1 domain + 1 action + 0-2 technology + 0-1 free-form. Minimum 2, maximum 5.
rules: Prefer taxonomy terms. Use free-form only when no taxonomy term fits.
At least 1 domain tag and 1 action tag per node.
Technology tags are optional but helpful for search precision.

taggingExamples.scenario: Added a new REST endpoint for user profiles
Decided to use Redis for session caching instead of in-memory
Fixed N+1 query in the dashboard loader
Migrated Dockerfile from alpine to distroless
Investigated memory leak in WebSocket handler

splitting.description: How to split a coding session into multiple NodeSeeds.
guidelines: One node per distinct knowledge unit: a decision made, a pattern discovered, a bug found and fixed, a new capability added.
Do NOT create nodes for trivial changes (typo fixes, import reordering, whitespace cleanup).
Merge closely related changes into a single node. 'Fixed auth + added auth tests' = one node, not two.
If a session has only one notable change, extract one node. Do not pad.
Prefer fewer high-quality nodes over many shallow ones.

splittingExamples.sessionSummary: Added dark mode toggle, created theme CSS variables, stored preference in localStorage, fixed contrast issues in sidebar
splittingExamples.reason: All changes serve one feature (dark mode). One node.

splittingExamples.sessionSummary: Implemented volunteer queue for extraction, also fixed a JWT refresh bug, and updated docker-compose for new service
splittingExamples.reason: Three unrelated changes: queue implementation, auth bugfix, infra config.

splittingExamples.sessionSummary: Investigated performance issue, discovered N+1 query, decided to add DataLoader pattern, implemented batch resolver
splittingExamples.reason: Investigation + discovery = one node. Implementation of the solution = another.

summaryWriting.description: Summary quality is critical — it determines the node's spatial position (via vectorization) and identity (SHA256 hash for dedup).
rules: Start with a verb or noun phrase describing WHAT happened.
Include the key technology or component name for search precision.
Avoid vague phrases: 'Updated code' → 'Replaced polling with WebSocket push for real-time status updates'.
Avoid session-specific language: 'Today I fixed...' → 'Fixed race condition in connection pool cleanup'.
good: Implemented pull-based volunteer queue for distributed LLM extraction in Gateway
Chose Dual Timeout pattern (idle + maxWait) for IngestBuffer batching
Fixed JWT token refresh to auto-renew before session expiry with 5-minute window
Discovered that PostgreSQL NOTIFY has 8000-byte payload limit, switched to polling for large messages
bad: Updated the code (too vague — what code? what change?)
Worked on authentication stuff (not a knowledge unit)
Today I spent 3 hours debugging (session narrative, not knowledge)

contentWriting.description: Content is the L3 detail visible when a user focuses on the node. Optional but valuable for non-trivial changes.
rules: Include implementation details, rationale, and gotchas.
Write for a developer who will encounter the same problem months later.
Keep under 500 characters. Be dense with information.
Omit for trivial or self-explanatory changes.

capsuleStructure.description: Your output gets wrapped into an ExperienceCapsule. Understanding tiers helps you prioritize.

tiers.topTier: Max 2 nodes. High-value discoveries: architectural decisions, design patterns, critical bugfixes. Gets higher initial weight in Sphere. Use sparingly.
tiers.normalNodes: Max 10 nodes. Standard knowledge units. Most of your output goes here.
tiers.ghostNodes: Max 3 nodes. Low-confidence or aspirational notes: things you noticed but couldn't verify, TODO items discovered, potential issues spotted. Short TTL in Sphere.

tierAssignment: topTier: Decisions that affect project architecture, security policies, API contracts, performance-critical patterns.
normalNodes: Implementation details, bugfixes, configuration changes, dependency updates, test strategies.
ghostNodes: 'Noticed X might be a problem', 'Should investigate Y', 'Potential optimization in Z'. Hunches and leads, not certainties.

outputFormat.description: Return a JSON object with tier arrays. No text outside the JSON.

topTier.summary: Chose pull-based volunteer queue over push-based Extractor as primary extraction path
topTier.content: Push-based IngestBuffer sends all raw data to server-side Extractor (costs API credits). Pull-based VolunteerQueue lets idle LLM clients claim work items — zero cost. Extractor becomes fallback only (10min timeout).

normalNodes.summary: Implemented VolunteerQueue with enqueue/poll/submit lifecycle and FIFO ordering
normalNodes.content: In-memory queue with states: pending → claimed → completed → Digestor. Reaper handles timeouts. Overflow protection at 100 items.

normalNodes.summary: Fixed JWT token refresh to auto-renew before session expiry with 5-minute window
normalNodes.content: Token middleware checks expiry before each API call, triggers background refresh if within 5 minutes.

ghostNodes.summary: VolunteerQueue may need rate limiting per user to prevent abuse

antiPatterns: Do NOT assign h/w/d scores — Packer handles initial metrics from tier classification.
Do NOT create a node for every file touched. One node per knowledge unit, not per file.
Do NOT include session narrative ('I spent 2 hours...'). Extract the knowledge, not the story.
Do NOT create nodes for changes you don't understand. If the session text is unclear, extract fewer nodes.
Do NOT fabricate content beyond what the session text describes.
